Name: agent-auth
Description: Token-based authorization system for gating AI agent access to host applications.

Name: agent-auth
Description: HTTP server and CLI for token lifecycle management, validation, and JIT approval running on the host.

Name: agent-auth
Description: HTTP server and CLI providing token lifecycle management, scope-based authorization, JIT approval, and audit logging.
Functions: Create Token Pair, Refresh Token Pair, Revoke Token Family, Modify Token Family Scopes, Rotate Token Family, Introspect Token, Detect Refresh Token Reuse, Verify Token Signature, Check Token Expiry, Check Scope Authorization, Resolve Access Tier, Request Approval, Load Notification Plugin, Record Approval Grant, Check Existing Grant, Expire Grants, Encrypt Field, Decrypt Field, Serve Validate Endpoint, Serve Refresh Endpoint, Serve Reissue Endpoint, Serve Status Endpoint, Log Token Operation, Log Authorization Decision, Handle Token Create Command, Handle Token List Command, Handle Token Modify Command, Handle Token Revoke Command, Handle Token Rotate Command, Handle Serve Command

Name: SQLite
Description: Embedded relational database providing persistent storage for token families, tokens, and approval grants.
Functions: Store Token Family, Store Token, Mark Token Consumed, Mark Family Revoked, Store Approval Grant, Query Tokens

Name: keyring
Description: Python library providing a unified API for accessing system keyring backends to store the HMAC signing key and AES-256-GCM encryption key.
Functions: Generate Signing Key, Load Signing Key, Generate Encryption Key, Load Encryption Key

Name: macOS Keychain
Description: macOS system keyring backend used to securely store the HMAC signing key and database encryption key.

Name: gnome-keyring
Description: Linux system keyring backend (via libsecret/D-Bus Secret Service) used to securely store the HMAC signing key and database encryption key.

Name: example-app-bridge
Description: HTTP server running on the host that proxies operations to an external system, delegating authorization to agent-auth.

Name: example-app-bridge
Description: HTTP server that maps endpoints to external system interactions, delegating token validation and JIT approval to agent-auth.
Functions: Delegate Token Validation, Execute External System Interaction, Serve Bridge HTTP API

Name: example-app-cli
Description: Thin CLI client for interacting with an external system via its bridge, runnable from host or devcontainer.

Name: example-app-cli
Description: CLI client that sends authenticated HTTP requests to the app bridge, with automatic token refresh and re-issuance on 401 responses.
Functions: Send Bridge Request, Auto Refresh Token, Store CLI Credentials, Handle App Commands, Display Results

Name: keyring
Description: Python library providing a unified API for accessing system keyring backends to store CLI credentials.

Name: macOS Keychain
Description: macOS system keyring backend used to securely store CLI credentials.

Name: gnome-keyring
Description: Linux system keyring backend (via libsecret/D-Bus Secret Service) used to securely store CLI credentials.
